# **Pharmacology**

## **Pharmacokinetics and Dynamics**

* Pharmacokinetics is the L1 study of how the body affects a drug (Absorption, Distribution, Metabolism, Excretion) | Kinetics | 0.96  
* Pharmacodynamics is the L1 study of how a drug affects the body, primarily through L0 drug-receptor interactions | Dynamics | 0.96  
* Bioavailability is the L0 fraction of an administered drug that reaches the systemic circulation unchanged | Kinetics | 0.95  
* The Half-life (![][image1]) of a drug is the L0 time required for its plasma concentration to decrease by 50% | Kinetics | 0.95  
* Agonists are ligands that bind to receptors and initiate an L1 biological response | Dynamics | 0.94  
* Antagonists bind to receptors but do not initiate a response, acting as L1 "Inhibitory Gates" | Dynamics | 0.94

## **Mechanisms and Signaling**

* The Therapeutic Index is the L3 ratio of the dose that produces toxicity to the dose that produces a clinically desired effect | Safety | 0.92  
* Drug-receptor binding is a biological implementation of "Signal and Channel," where the drug is the message | Information Theory | 0.93  
* First-pass metabolism is the L0 process where drug concentration is greatly reduced before reaching systemic circulation | Physiology | 0.94  
* Synergism occurs when the combined L0 effect of two drugs is greater than the sum of their individual effects | Interaction | 0.91

## **Pharmacology Meta-Context**

* Dose-response curves represent the L1 mathematical model of L0 physiological saturation | Statistics | 0.95  
* Enzyme induction/inhibition represents an L1 feedback loop that alters the metabolism of other Grains/Drugs | Cybernetics | 0.93  
* Placebo effects represent an L2 narrative influence on L0 biological outcomes | Psychology | 0.88  
* Pharmacogenomics uses L1 genetic data to predict L0 drug responses, bridging Biology and Pharmacology | Genetics | 0.94

[image1]: <data:image/png;base64,>
